sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Segment connector** integrates with Twilio Segment via the Public API v1. It enables Shielva to sync sources, spaces, and functions from your Segment workspace into the knowledge base.

This connector uses **Bearer token** authentication — a single Public API access token sent as `Authorization: Bearer {token}` on every request.

**Key capabilities:**
- Validate the access token via `GET /workspaces` on install and health check
- Sync all sources with cursor-based pagination (handles workspaces of any size)
- Sync Profiles AI spaces and custom functions
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256("source:" + resource_id)[:16] for deduplication across syncs
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off for transient errors and rate limits
- Circuit breaker (threshold: 5 failures, 60s recovery) for health degradation detection

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates access_token by calling GET /workspaces; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE with reason |
| `health_check()` | Calls GET /workspaces to verify token; returns workspace name in message |
| `sync()` | Fetches all sources (cursor-paginated) + spaces + functions; normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_workspaces()` | GET /workspaces — returns single workspace for this token |
| `list_sources(pagination_cursor)` | Cursor-paginated GET /sources |
| `get_source(source_id)` | GET /sources/{source_id} for a single source |
| `list_destinations(source_id)` | GET /sources/{source_id}/destinations — all destinations for a source |
| `list_spaces()` | GET /spaces — all Profiles AI spaces |
| `list_functions(pagination_cursor)` | Cursor-paginated GET /functions |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The connector uses **Bearer token** authentication. The Public API access token is sent as `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}` on every request.

**How to obtain a token:**
1. Log in to [app.segment.com](https://app.segment.com)
2. Go to Settings → Access Management → Tokens
3. Click **Create Token** — choose Workspace Member (read) or higher
4. Copy the token immediately — it is shown only once

**Install fields:**

| Field | Key | Type | Required |
|-------|-----|------|----------|
| Public API Access Token | `access_token` | password | Yes |

**Validation endpoint:** `GET /workspaces` — called during install and health check.

children.title: Required Token Scopes
children.content: The connector requires at minimum **Workspace Member** role (read access). Workspace Owner is recommended for full access to all resource types including functions.

sections.title: Sync Behavior
sections.content:
The `sync()` method fetches three resource types in order:

1. **Sources** — cursor-paginated via `pagination[cursor]`; follows all pages until `pagination.next` is null
2. **Spaces** — single request; failures are non-fatal
3. **Functions** — cursor-paginated; failures are non-fatal

Each resource is normalized to a `ConnectorDocument` and optionally pushed to a knowledge base via `_ingest_document()`.

**Return values:**
- `SyncStatus.COMPLETED` — zero document failures
- `SyncStatus.PARTIAL` — some documents failed to normalize
- `SyncStatus.FAILED` — the sources API returned a fatal error (auth failure, etc.)

children.title: Cursor Pagination
Document ID Stability
children.content: Each document receives a stable 16-character hex ID: `SHA-256("source:" + resource_id)[:16]`. This ensures the same Segment resource always maps to the same Shielva document ID across syncs, enabling upsert deduplication.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
The connector defines a typed exception hierarchy:

| Exception | Trigger | Retried? |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| `SegmentAuthError` | 401 / 403 | No — requires human intervention |
| `SegmentRateLimitError` | 429 | Yes — up to 3 attempts with `Retry-After` honour |
| `SegmentNetworkError` | Timeout / connection refused | Yes — exponential back-off |
| `SegmentNotFoundError` | 404 | Yes (treated as transient) |
| `SegmentServerError` | 5xx | Yes — exponential back-off |
| `SegmentError` | All other HTTP errors | Yes |

children.title: Retry Policy
children.content:
- Max attempts: 3
- Base delay: 1.0 s
- Backoff factor: 2× per attempt
- Jitter: ±0.5 s random
- Max delay: 30 s
- Auth errors are never retried

children.title: Circuit Breaker
children.content:
- Opens after 5 consecutive failures
- Recovery timeout: 60 s
- Half-open: single probe request after timeout
- Reflected in `health_check()` as `ConnectorHealth.DEGRADED` or `OFFLINE`

sections.title: API Reference
sections.content: All methods are async and return typed dataclasses or raw API dicts.

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates the access token by calling `GET /workspaces`.

**Returns:** `InstallResult`
- `health`: `HEALTHY` on success, `OFFLINE` on failure
- `auth_status`: `CONNECTED`, `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`, `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`, or `FAILED`
- `message`: workspace name on success, error description on failure
- `connector_id`: populated on success

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Calls `GET /workspaces` and returns the current connectivity state.

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult`
- `health`: `HEALTHY`, `DEGRADED`, or `OFFLINE`
- `message`: `"Connected to Segment — {workspace_name}"` on success

children.title: sync(full=False, since=None, kb_id='')
children.content:
Full sync across sources, spaces, and functions.

**Returns:** `SyncResult`
- `status`: `COMPLETED`, `PARTIAL`, or `FAILED`
- `documents_found`: total resources discovered
- `documents_synced`: successfully normalized
- `documents_failed`: failed to normalize

children.title: list_sources(pagination_cursor=None)
children.content:
Returns one page of sources. Pass `pagination_cursor` from the previous response's `data.pagination.next` to fetch the next page.

**Returns:** raw Segment API dict

children.title: get_source(source_id)
children.content:
Retrieves a single source by ID.

**Returns:** raw Segment API dict with `data.source` key

children.title: list_destinations(source_id)
children.content:
Returns all destinations connected to a specific source.

**Returns:** raw Segment API dict with `data.destinations` list

children.title: list_spaces()
children.content:
Returns all Profiles AI spaces in the workspace.

**Returns:** raw Segment API dict with `data.spaces` list

children.title: list_functions(pagination_cursor=None)
children.content:
Returns one page of functions. Supports cursor pagination.

**Returns:** raw Segment API dict with `data.functions` list and `data.pagination`

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `access_token is required` | No token provided | Enter the Public API token in the install form |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Invalid or revoked token | Re-generate at Segment → Settings → Access Management → Tokens |
| `403 Forbidden` | Insufficient token scope | Ensure Workspace Member role or higher |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Rate limit exceeded | Connector retries automatically; wait and retry if all attempts exhaust |
| No sources returned | Empty workspace or wrong token | Verify the workspace has sources and token belongs to correct workspace |
